name: Network Topology & Architecture
description: Sub-sector template for wireless network topology patents. Covers mesh networks, relay architectures, heterogeneous networks (HetNet), device-to-device (D2D) communication, and non-terrestrial network (NTN) integration.
contextDescription: Network topology patents cover how network nodes are organized and interconnect. The trend is toward heterogeneous, multi-tier architectures: macro + small cell + relay + IAB (Integrated Access and Backhaul). 5G-Advanced introduces non-terrestrial networks (NTN) with satellite integration. D2D (sidelink) communication enables V2X and public safety applications. Key targets: infrastructure vendors, mesh network companies (for enterprise/industrial), satellite operators (Starlink, OneWeb), and automotive (C-V2X). Topology patents often have broad architectural scope.
scoringGuidance: HetNet topology patents affect every operator's densification strategy.
IAB (Integrated Access and Backhaul) is critical for 5G mmWave — self-backhauling small cells.
NTN (satellite) integration with terrestrial 5G is a high-growth area — 3GPP Rel-17+.
D2D/sidelink patents cover V2X (vehicle-to-everything) — major automotive market.
Mesh and relay patents are relevant to enterprise private 5G deployments.

questions.question: What network topology does this patent address? Score 8-10 for IAB (integrated access and backhaul) or NTN (satellite-terrestrial integration), 5-7 for HetNet or relay architectures, 3-4 for mesh or ad-hoc, 1-2 for traditional single-tier.
questions.reasoningPrompt: IAB and NTN are the newest and highest-value topology innovations in 5G.

questions.question: Does this patent address device-to-device (D2D) or sidelink communication (V2X, ProSe)? Score 8-10 for C-V2X or NR sidelink innovations, 5-7 for general D2D communication, 3-4 for proximity-based services, 1-2 for no D2D aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: V2X is a major market — every connected vehicle will use sidelink.

questions.question: Does this patent address non-terrestrial network integration (satellite-terrestrial, HAPS, drone-based)? Score 8-10 for novel NTN-terrestrial integration, 5-7 for satellite communication with 3GPP alignment, 3-4 for general aerial/satellite concepts, 1-2 for terrestrial only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: NTN is standardized in 3GPP Rel-17+ — satellite direct-to-device is a major trend.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP network architecture specifications (TS 23.501, TS 38.300)? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of standardized architecture, 5-7 for standard-compatible, 3-4 for standard-adjacent, 1-2 for proprietary.
questions.reasoningPrompt: 3GPP defines network architecture — SEP-aligned topology patents affect all implementers.

questions.question: How well does this topology scale (number of nodes, geographic coverage, user density)? Score 8-10 for massive scale (1000+ nodes, nationwide), 5-7 for metropolitan scale, 3-4 for enterprise/campus, 1-2 for point-to-point only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Higher scalability = more deployment scenarios = broader licensing scope.

questions.question: How detectable is this topology innovation in a deployed network? Score 8-10 for observable in network architecture (node types, interconnections), 5-7 for detectable via signaling analysis, 3-4 for internal network management, 1-2 for conceptual only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Network topology is often visible — cell types, relay nodes, and backhaul links can be observed.
